evals.prompt: I'm setting up hiring for NovaTech Solutions, a mid-size fintech company in Singapore with about 200 employees. We're looking for a Head of Platform Engineering. Our team is scaling fast and we need someone who can manage a team of 15 engineers and drive our cloud migration.
evals.expected_output: Creates a new hiring context with company details, confirms context_id. Since no CV files are available, acknowledges that and suggests next steps. Creates a JD from the description provided.

assertions.name: creates_context
assertions.description: Calls atlas_create_context with company name, industry, size, and location

assertions.name: creates_jd
assertions.description: Calls atlas_create_jd with job title and description content

assertions.name: presents_next_steps
assertions.description: Mentions next steps like uploading candidates or running analysis

evals.prompt: Do I already have a hiring context set up? I think I created one before for a tech company.
evals.expected_output: Lists existing contexts, finds the CareerProof context with 14 candidates, presents what's already there including candidate count and JD.

assertions.name: lists_contexts
assertions.description: Calls atlas_list_contexts to check for existing contexts

assertions.name: shows_existing_data
assertions.description: Reports the existing context with candidate count and JD title

assertions.name: no_duplicate_creation
assertions.description: Does NOT create a new context when one already exists
